market_context: U.S. equity futures are essentially flat to slightly positive pre-market, with S&P 500 futures up 0.03%, Nasdaq-100 futures up 0.1%, and Dow futures up 0.02%.[1] The VIX is relatively subdued at 16.35 and the 10-year Treasury yield remains elevated at 4.61%, which favors a modestly cautious, range-bound session rather than a strong risk-on breakout.[1]

predictions.reasoning: SPY futures were slightly positive pre-market and SPY itself was already indicated up 0.07% in the pre-open session.[1] With volatility muted and yields stable, the most likely outcome is a small grind higher rather than a sharp directional move.

Nasdaq-100 futures outperformed the broad market slightly, rising 0.1% pre-market, and QQQ was already up 0.29% in the cited pre-bell read.[1] That setup supports continued relative strength in large-cap tech, especially if bond yields do not spike further.

The session tone favors AI/mega-cap tech, and Nasdaq futures were modestly firmer ahead of the open.[1] NVDA should benefit more than the average large-cap if traders continue to buy the strongest AI leaders on a low-volatility day.

Tesla often trades with higher beta than the index, so in a low-conviction tape it is more vulnerable to fading than the broader market. The flat futures backdrop and elevated yields do not provide a strong catalyst for sustained upside.
